observations.recommendation:
The Municipal Accountant trace the root cause of the accounting errors and effect the necessary adjusting entries and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor; and 

Prospectively, the Accounting Office be more mindful of the accounts used to journalize the transactions and the eventual result of such transactions in order to properly present the accounts’ balances.

Management instruct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, to identify the projects covering the balances of Advances to Contractors. Also, to exert more efforts to fast track and exhaust all administrative and legal means for the immediate recovery of long outstanding advances to contractors. Furthermore, require the contractors concerned to refund the unrecouped amount for terminated and completed projects or deduct the amount from progress billings of other existing contracts of the said contractors.

Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Continue the reconciliation to determine the legitimacy of the transactions, and if found to be valid claims, communicate with the concerned payees for the payment/settlement of the accounts;

Revert to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund those that proved to be with no valid claims; and,

Conduct periodic review and monitoring of the Accounts Payable account so that this account will consist only of valid claims against the LGU.

The Municipal General Services Officer, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor, provide the Municipal Accountant with a copy of the title of 11 parcels of land and/or obtain available documents relative to said parcels of land to determine the status of the same.

Furthermore, Management to initiate steps to effect the registration of the LGU properties under the Torrens Title System.

Management reminded its responsible officers the following:

General Services Officer (GSO):

Property Unit – Physically segregate and promptly list down all obsolete and unserviceable PPE with complete data in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP), duly signed by the Municipal Accountant, Inspector and Appraisal Committee, and approved by the Head of the Agency or authorized representative, and submit to the Disposal Committee for disposal as early as possible; and,

Disposal Committee – immediately dispose of all unserviceable/ obsolete PPEs using the appropriate mode of disposal as determined and recommended by the Disposal Committee, to avoid further deterioration, earn income from its sale, if warranted, and make use of the storage spaces occupied by these items.

Municipal Accountant – require the GSO to submit the necessary documents together with the Official Receipt after disposal, as the basis to drop the unserviceable properties from the books of accounts to come up with the accurate balance of the PPE account in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 89-296 and 92-386.

The Municipal Mayor to direct:

the LDRRM Officers Mantos and Clavedo to provide documentation on the release of the goods to recipients;

the Municipal Budget Officer provide justification on the release of funds from 30% despite the low implementation rate of the 70% portion; and

the OIC-LDRRM Officer to follow strictly the regulation in the utilization of the 30% QRF of the 5% LDRRMF and properly plan for the PPAs in the LDRRMIF that are essential in the Municipality.

Moreover, the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant check the suitability of the procurement whether it is proper to charge against to the 30% QRF.

The Municipal Mayor:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with Municipal Treasurer in identifying the employees with unremitted contributions to enable the Municipality to settle the obligations; and 

Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a schedule or subsidiary ledger and record the amounts in the proper account to have accurate and reliable Financial Statements.

The Municipal Mayor:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with Municipal Treasurer in identifying the employees with unremitted contributions to enable the Municipality to settle the obligations; and 

Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare schedule or subsidiary ledger and record the amounts in the proper account to have accurate and reliable Financial Statements.

The Municipal Mayor:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with Municipal Treasurer in identifying the employees with unremitted contributions to enable the Municipality to settle the obligations; and

Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare schedule or subsidiary ledger and record the amounts in the proper account to have accurate and reliable Financial Statements.

The MCPC Focal Person properly establish the disaggregated database which is monitored and updated yearly to come up with more responsive child centric PPAs that will help address the related issues and concerns.
The concerned Municipal Department Head refund the overtime pay given which is contrary to CSC and DBM Joint Circular No. 01 s. 2015 dated 25 November 2015.

The Local Chief Executive and the former OIC-LDRRM Officer provide justification why the two aforementioned incidents cannot not be considered as a deviation on the use of the Rescue Vehicle from its intended LDRRMF purpose; and

Management refrain from using LDRRM properties, which were procured to be exclusively used for response and rescue/relief activities be only used for such purpose.

Management provide a garage that will shelter these LGU acquired vehicles away from the natural elements to prolong its useful life. These can also be transferred to other areas so as to keep the properties from theft or misuse.

Management, through the General Services Officer, submit to the Agency Auditors the Driver’s Trip Tickets, Monthly Report of Official Travel or Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption surrounding the ₱ 1.410 million worth of procurements;

Management, through the General Services Officer, improve the internal control surrounding the issuance of fuel, oil and lubricants to end users. Procurement of fuel tank is to be considered in order to provide storage container for fuel to facilitate the release of the inventory and for proper monitoring of the utilization;

The GAD Focal Person:

Gather and build up sex-disaggregated data and GAD related information that can be sourced in the agencies suggested by the Circular such as the NSO, NSCB, DSWD and Dep ED. Also, maintain and establish GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation;

Update of the GAD codes or ordinances to support the LGU in promoting, protecting and fulfilling women’s human rights, women economic empowerment and gender-responsive governance towards the attainment of gender equality and women’s empowerment; and

Ensure the effective and efficient implementation of the GAD PPAs and the judicious utilization of the GAD Budget.

The Municipal Mayor:

Create a special committee, headed by the Municipal Accountant, to craft the internal rules on the use of digital signature or other types of electronic signature on electronic documents. The internal rules shall include provisions with regards to the sanctions for unauthorized and illegal use of digital certificates or electronic signatures; and

Submit the Management Representation or Policy Statement on the use of signature on electronic documents in their operations to their respective Auditors, together with the approved internal rules.

Management adopt policies and internal controls to monitor loan accommodations and other payroll deductions such that the monthly net take home pay of any employee does not fall below the prescribed ceiling of ₱5,000.00 as provided under Section 49 of RA 11518.

Likewise, submit the documents enumerated in Items 3.1.2 and 3.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2009-001.

Management promptly implement PPAs to ensure that socio-economic benefits expected to be provided by these projects will be enjoyed by constituents within the budget year and ensure continuous development in the community.

The Municipal Mayor with the technical assistance of the Municipal Engineer Issue an executive order creating the CPES-IU, identifying its members and responsibilities or integrating the CPES function in an existing unit with similar functions which shall act as the CPES-IU in accordance with CPES implementing Guidelines for Infrastructure Projects; Formulate and adopt specific implementing guidelines from the evaluation of project types which do not have specific CPES guidelines; and Ensure the proper training of personnel involved in CPES Implementation.

We recommend to the Municipal Accounting Office, through the Municipal Accountant, to submit immediately the required supporting documents for the transactions mentioned to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.

The Sangguniang Bayan Members through its presiding officer shall prioritize the utilization of the long overdue appropriation before approving its supplemental budget and 

The LCE shall: 

request to the Sangguniang Bayan the reversion of the balances of the continuing appropriations those obligations that have been fully paid or settled and 

impose the implementation on the continuing programs/projects and procurement of different equipment, furniture and fixtures so that the benefits to be derived therefrom could bolster more improvement and development of the municipal government.

We recommended the following: 

The Local Chief Executive, through the GAD Focal Person shall:

include in the Gender and Development Plan all different gender issues of the various sectors of the Municipality based on their priority needs and concerns; and

ensure the effective and efficient implementation of the GAD PPAs and the judicious utilization of the GAD Budget.

Local Chief Executive shall issue an Executive Order or Administrative Order creating the Monitoring and Evaluation Team to enhance, strengthen and/or maintain a gender responsive monitoring and evaluation system.

The Accounting Office retrace all taxes withheld and the corresponding taxes paid and provide for the adjusting entries for the discrepancies noted; and

The Treasurer’s Office cause the immediate remittance of all taxes withheld in accordance with existing rules and regulations issued by the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) and other implementing agencies to avoid the misuse of said funds and the incurrence of unnecessary expenses in the form of interest and surcharges.

The Municipal Accountant comply with the preparation and submission of the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements within the reglementary period as required under COA Circular No.  96-011 dated October 2. 1996 to ascertain the existence, validity and accuracy of the LGUs Cash in Bank Balance, ensure the fair presentation of accounts in the Financial Statements; and avoid legal sanctions as provided in Section 3.9 of same circular;

Require the submission of supporting documents and/or compliance of requirements as indicated in the respective Audit Observation Memorandum (AOM) and/or Notice of Suspension (NS) issued to facilitate proper disposition of subject transaction documents and that vouchers and payrolls onwards be duly processed and properly documented before they will be approved and paid to facilitate proper recording in the books of accounts and avoid possible suspension and/or disallowance in audit.

Require the submission of the certification signed by the requisitioning officer that the drug products being requisitioned or procured fall within and conform with PNDF Volume I, Current Edition and let this be serve as a warning pursuant to rule 76 of the IRR for 1st violation.

All damaged, destroyed parts and tires due for replacement shall be pre-inspected to determine whether such replacement is necessary.

Waste Material Report should be prepared and turn over the replaced parts and tires to the Municipal Treasurer for storage and proper disposition.

Conduct the post-repair inspection on the replaced parts and tires to ascertain its installation and performance.

Require officials and employees concerned to ensure that:

Driver’s trip ticket is duly and properly accomplished and approved before undertaking the trip;

That all required reports such as Monthly Report of Official Travel and Report of Fuel Consumption are prepared and submitted regularly; and

That all other measures should be exercised by all concerned to prevent wasteful, excessive and unnecessary expenditures for fuel consumption.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant exert extra effort to trace and reconcile the balance of Cash in Bank accounts. Copies of bank debit and credit memoranda be secured at the earliest possible time and reconciliation with the depository bank be made periodically and immediately after receipt of the bank statements to timely adjust or correct the balances of the agency accounts. Documents from the bank be persistently secured to serve as basis for the adjustment/corrections and recording of charges made by the bank on the Municipality’s accounts.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct its General Services Officer and Supply Officers to conduct semestral inventory of supplies and prepare reports and submit the results of the inventory taking to the Municipal Accountant for the latter to prepare the necessary adjusting entries where appropriate, for the fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements. Furthermore, submit to the Auditors every six months the duly certified and approved Report of the Physical Count of Inventories for each type of inventory.
Moreover, instruct the custodians to maintain Stock Card for each type of supply to record all receipts and issuances and the Municipal Accountant to prepare its Subsidiary Ledger Cards. Likewise, regularly update the stock card of each item for the issuances made based on a summary of issuances made on a daily basis, and each time items are received to properly monitor against under/over stocking of supplies, and to establish control against theft or abuse of the inventory items. Consider providing a stock room or bodega for the GSO to store the supplies and materials and additional personnel to monitor the movement of the inventory items.
Also, direct the General Services Officer to submit the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) to the Municipal Accountant on a monthly basis for recording purposes and preparation of the corresponding JEV to record the supplies issued and make the necessary adjustments for the cost of unrecorded inventories based on the SSMI submitted.

Recommendation:
We recommended that:
As the subsidiaries of the discrepancies are already identified, the Accounting Office has to look into the reasons of the discrepancies and take up the necessary adjusting entries that will arise as a result. The Agency Auditors are to be furnished with copies of the JEV; and
The Accounting Office be more careful in the recording of transactions involving these types of transactions and ensure continuous reconciliation of the account balances.

Recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations as follows:
The Inventory Committee conduct an inventory of the local roads under the jurisdiction of the Municipality and render a Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System;
The General Services Officer:
Render a Report on Local Road Network and Road Map;
Regularly maintain and update the Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads; and
Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and
The Municipal Accountant:
Prepare and maintain a Local Road Network Ledger Card;
Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and
Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each of the depreciable component at the end of the year.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal General Services Officer to comply with the Property Insurance Law in insuring all serviceable properties with the GSIS and submit the PIF to the GSIS.

Recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
Instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, in coordination with the Municipal Development Council to include only projects that can be feasibly commenced and implemented during the year;
Instruct the BAC and Municipal Engineer to properly plan and schedule all procurement activities pertaining to development projects to pave the way for their timely implementation and completion; and
Ensure that the Local Development Council, Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer regularly monitor the status of project implementation so that necessary interventions may be introduced at the earliest time.

Recommendation:
We recommended the following:
The Local Chief Executive (LCE), through the OIC - Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer (MDRRMO) provide list of COVID-19 patient in connection with the use of the medicines and fuel; and
Implement fully the programs, projects and activities under Mitigation Fund through proper planning of the project timeline and/or schedule of implementation.
In addition, the LCE to require the LDRRM Council to constantly monitor the full implementation of the PPAs.

Recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the OIC-MDRRMO to prepare an updated LDRRMFIP to incorporate all the previous years’ unutilized PPAs and be made available for implementation.

Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately prepare and deposit the check for transfer of CYs 2020 and 2021 unexpended LDRRMF to STF and submit the CY 2022 DV of the transfer to STF to accounting office for recording in the books.
The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant revert to surplus the CYs 2015 to 2017 balances and be made available for other social services.

Management adopt policies and internal control measures to monitor loan accommodations and other payroll deductions such that the monthly net take home pay of any employee does not fall below the prescribed ceiling of ₱5,000.00 as provided under Section 55 of RA 11639. Among the policies to be included is for the Municipal Accountant to disapprove any loan application below the prescribed minimum take home pay.
Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to comply with the Property Insurance Law in insuring all serviceable properties with the GSIS.

Recommendation:
We recommended the following:
The Municipal Accountant, with the assistance of the Municipal Treasurer, identify the nature and status of the dormant accounts that are still subject of reconciliation;
The Municipal Treasurer revert back the balances to the Bureau of Treasury, the Source Agency or the general fund, as the case may be; and
The Municipal Treasurer monitor the activity or inactivity of its depository accounts and exert all efforts to prevent current and savings accounts from becoming dormant.

Consider the application of COA Circular No. 2016-005 on the possibility of writing off the receivables after considering the guidelines as provided for in IPSAS 29.

Create a special committee, headed by the Municipal Accountant, to craft the internal rules on the use of digital signature or other types of electronic signature on electronic documents. The internal rules shall include provisions with regards to the sanctions for unauthorized and illegal use of digital certificates or electronic signatures; and
Submit the Management Representation or Policy Statement on the use of signature on electronic documents in their operations to their respective Auditors, together with the approved internal rules.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management utilize and fully operationalize its recruitment process through its Human Resource Management Division (HRMD) to ensure that personnel who possess the qualification standards of funded plantilla positions are hired to achieve the purpose/s for which funds are appropriated. The Municipal HRMO uphold the priority needs, desired service requirement and financial capacity of the Local Government consistent with the principles of efficiency, economy and effectiveness.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management adopt policies and internal controls to monitor loan accommodations and other payroll deductions such that the monthly net take home pay of any employee does not fall below the prescribed ceiling of ₱5,000.00 as provided under Section 49 of RA 11518.
Likewise, submit the documents enumerated in Items 3.1.2 and 3.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2009-001.

The Municipal Mayor issue an executive order creating an Inventory Committee consistent with COA Circular No. 2020-006 who shall be momentarily relieved of all their regular duties to devote their full time in the conduct of the physical inventory taking until the same is completed;

The Inventory Committee conduct complete physical inventory of all property and submit the results thereof to the Agency Auditor;
The local school board maximize the utilization of the SEF through the implementation of the programs, activities and projects included in the SEF budget.
The Municipal Treasurer shall sign box B of the PR and not to release payments without verifying and ensuring that all requirements have been duly complied.

Management strictly follow the provisions of DSWD Order No. 22, as amended by Administrative Order No. 5, Series of 2008 and the submission of supporting documents and/or compliance of requirements as indicated to facilitate proper disposition of subject transaction documents and that vouchers onwards be duly processed and approved before they will be paid to facilitate proper recording in the books of accounts and avoid possible suspension and/or disallowance in audit.

Management to instruct the Municipal Engineer to prepare Program of Work and Detailed Estimates before implementation of the project and submit an Inspection Report and Certificate of Completion after completion of any infrastructure projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer cause the immediate submission of unsubmitted DVs complete with the necessary supporting documents, including the CY 2015 and 2016 to the Accounting Office. The Municipal Accountant, on the other hand, shall also submit these reports within ten (10) days after receipt to the Office of the Auditor for verification and post-audit. 

If warranted, the Municipal Mayor may institute suspension of payment of their salaries until they have complied with the aforesaid requirements pursuant to Section 122 (2) of PD. 1445.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation
We recommend that the Management and the Municipal Accountant should make a thorough verification of the items reflected in the bank reconciliation statements and identify the specific nature of the transactions lumped in the Unreconciled Amount item. The corresponding journal entry voucher should be drawn to effect the recording of the adjustments.

Recommendation
The Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer for the reconciliation of the unclaimed checks vis-à-vis bank statement and thereby present a verified schedule of the outstanding checks.

Recommendation
We recommend the following:
The Inventory Committee shall conduct complete physical inventory of all property and reconcile results with accounting records and require the Municipal Accountant to record any adjustments in the books. This includes the results of the inventory and recognition of the local road networks within the Municipality and submit the results thereof to the Agency Auditor;
The GSO - Designate shall prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report on all unserviceable properties that will be identified during the count and facilitate their immediate disposal to prevent further deterioration and decrease in salvage values; and
The OIC - Municipal Accountant shall recompute the depreciation on the Books account and furnish the Agency of the adjusting entry. Likewise, provide the justification behind the recognition of impairment losses in the books.

Recommendation
We recommend the following:
The Inventory Committee conduct an inventory of the local roads under the jurisdiction of the Municipality and render a Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System;
The Municipal Treasurer:
Render a Report on Local Road Network and Road Map;
Regularly maintain and update the Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads; and
Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components.
The Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects; and
The Municipal Accountant:
Prepare and maintain a Local Road Network Ledger Card;
Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and
Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.

Recommendations
We recommend that:
the Local Chief Executive prioritize the adequate staffing of qualified personnel on essential municipal departments, including the accounting office, in order to achieve the desirable office environment, such as proper record-keeping, completeness of the supporting documents and the like, and the submission of required financial reports within the reglementary period, especially in the finance departments; and
The Accounting Office to submit the financial documents and reports within the reglementary period.

Management Comment
Although it is noted that there is a lack of manpower in the offices, Management would do its best to make the submissions of the reports within the timelines set by the guidelines.

Numerous DVs including their supporting documents which were paid in checks for CY 2015 and CY 2016 remained submitted to the Office of the Auditor, on top of the delayed submission of the CY 2018 accounts contrary to COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995 and Section 122 of PD No. 1445, precluding review and examination thereof.
Pertinent provisions of Section 6.03 COA Circular No. 95-06 dated May 18, 1995 provide that:
“The official involved in the daily recording of transactions in the books of accounts shall turn over the receipts and the disbursement records with all paid vouchers and documents evidencing the transactions to the Auditor within ten (10) days from date of receipt of said documents.”
Section 122 of PD No. 1445, also states:
“Submission of reports. Whenever deemed necessary in the exigencies of the service, the Commission may under regulations issued by it require the agency heads, chief accountants, budget officers, cashiers, disbursing officers, administrative or personnel officers, and other responsible officials of the various agencies to submit trial balances, physical inventory reports, current plantilla of personnel, and such other reports as may be necessary for the exercise of its functions.
(2) Failure on the part of the officials concerned to submit the documents and reports mentioned herein shall automatically cause the suspension of payments of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements of the Commission.
For Calendar Year 2018, the Municipality’s submission of accounts and transaction documents disclosed that a considerable number of disbursements vouchers and their supporting documents which were paid in checks were not yet submitted to the Office of the Auditor contrary to the above provisions precluding review and examination thereof.
Also, there were no substantial actions taken on the status of the unsubmitted transaction documents and financial reports for the CYs 2015 and 2016 (Appendix “I-1 and I-2”), as contained in our Annual Audit Report for the CYs 2015 and 2016.
Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code, provides:
“Failure of accountable officer to render accounts- Any public officer, whether in the service or separated therefrom by resignation or any other cause, who is required by law and regulation to render to the Insular Auditor, or to a provincial auditor and who fails to do so for a period of two months after accounts should be rendered, shall be punished by prison correctional in its minimum period, or by a fine ranging from 200 to 6,000 pesos, or both.”
Prompt and complete submission of accounts is encouraged to facilitate an early review and examination of the transactions covered therein. Unless these accounts are submitted and subsequently reviewed, the legality, validity, and propriety of these transactions will not be ascertained. Errors, if any, will not be detected, hence, adjustments/corrections in the books could not be timely made.
Management Comment:
The CY 2018 accounts were already submitted.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
Although DVs for CY 2018 were already submitted, material amounts for CY 2015 and CY 2016 transactions remained not submitted and little was done to substantially reduce the same.
Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer cause the immediate submission of unsubmitted DVs complete with the necessary supporting documents, including the CY 2015 and 2016 to the Accounting Office. The Municipal Accountant, on the other hand, shall also submit these reports within ten (10) days after receipt to the Office of the Auditor for verification and post-audit.
If warranted, the Municipal Mayor may institute suspension of payment of their salaries until they have complied with the aforesaid requirements pursuant to Section 122 (2) of PD. 1445.

Recommendations
We recommend that:
Management, headed by the Municipal Mayor, plan better the projects that would be charged to specific funds in order to reduce the chances of projects identified to the wrong fund resulting to its non-implementation; and
Ensure that the projects identified should be implemented during the year so that the socio-economic development and environmental targets would be achieved.

Management Comment
Management commented that the reason of the delay in the implementation was caused by the delay in the receipt of the approval from the Sangguniang Bayan for the purchase of the fire truck while the road maintenance aspect is supposedly one of road rehabilitation.

Recommendation:
We recommend the following:
The Municipal Budget Officer:
in approving the Obligation Request (ObR), shall adhere strictly to the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units. Other General Services account should not include expenses where in nature doing administrative matters and/or functioning clerical services; and
stop the practice of charging Other General Services under MOOE account for all future payments of salaries and wages of casual and contractual employees doing administrative matters and/or functioning clerical services in order to circumvent the rule on PS limitation as provided in RA 7160.
The Local Chief Executive, before signing the contract under personnel hired as Contract of Services, shall ensure that the salary rate of the concerned personnel are pre-determined by the Sangguniang Bayan following the municipality’s standard salary rate.

Management Comment
Management will comply the implementations effective 01 June 2019 as agreed.

Recommendation:
We recommend the following:
The Local Chief Executive, through the GAD Focal Person shall:
include in the Gender and Development Plan all different gender issues of the various sectors of the Municipality based on their priority needs and concerns; and
ensure the effective and efficient implementation of the GAD PPAs and the judicious utilization of the GAD Budget.
Local Chief Executive shall issue an Executive Order or Administrative Order creating the Monitoring and Evaluation Team to enhance, strengthen and/or maintain a gender responsive monitoring and evaluation system.

Management Comment
Management promised to comply with the audit recommendation.

Recommendation
We recommend that Management should observe strict compliance with the relevant provisions of law and guidelines especially on the accounting and reporting of LDRRMF for government resources to be assured of having been administered and used properly, regularly, and legally according to the purposes for which the fund was created.
More specifically, we recommend the following:
The OIC - Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers for the transfers of agency’s unutilized DRRMF to the Special Trust Fund;
Municipal Treasurer to revert the unexpended Trust Fund balances which are already beyond five years and the OIC-Municipal Accountant to properly record the reversion back to the General Fund in order for the cash to be made available for other social services of the municipality;
Unexpended Trust Fund cash balances for CY 2015, 2016 and 2017 should be transferred to the Trust Fund Account; and
The OIC-Municipal Accountant to adjust the balance of the Trust Liabilities- DRRMF to reflect the true balance of the LDRRMF Accounts.

Management Comment
Management has promised to comply with the audit recommendations.

Report on the Sources and Utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) and other dedicated disaster risk reduction and management resources for the CY 2018 were not submitted to the Auditor’s office as required under Section 4, Rule 6 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of Republic Act (RA) 10121 and item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
Item 25 of Section 4, Rule 6 of the IRR of RA 10121, otherwise known as the Philippine Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Act of 2010 provides that:
“Section 4. Functions. The provincial, city, and municipal DRRMOs or BDRRMCs, in coordination with concerned national agencies and instrumentalities, shall perform the following functions with impartiality, given the emerging challenges bought by disaster of our times:
Xxx
(25) Prepare and submit through the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council and the Local Disaster Council, the report on the utilization of the LDDRMF and other dedicated disaster risk reduction management resources to the Local Commission on Audit (COA) for provincial/ city level and internal audit for municipal and component city level, copy furnished the regional director of the OCD and the Local Government Operations Officer of the DILG; and
Xxx”
Item 5.15 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated 9/12/12 provides for the form and details of the report stated as follows:
“A Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the format in Annex B shall be prepared and certified correct by the Local Accountant. The Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) shall submit the report on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC) to the COA auditor of the LGU.”
Non- submission of the monthly report run counter with the established law and circular aforementioned and delayed the team’s audit and evaluation of the agency’s utilization of the fund particularly in the aspect of determining whether the disbursements were responsive to the objectives of the LDRRMF.
Recommendation
We recommend that Management direct the timely submission of the monthly Report on the Utilization of the LDRRMF by the MDRRMO- designate as certified correct by the Municipal Accountant to the Office of the Auditor as required under item 25, Section 4, Rule 6 of IRR of RA 10121 and detailed under item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

Management Comment
Management will comply with the audit recommendation.

Recommendations
We recommend that:
Management, particularly the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and the Municipal Accountant’s Office, submit an explanation and/or justification on the noted discrepancies between the account balances and balances per audit;
The Accounting Office retrace all taxes withheld and the corresponding taxes paid and provide for the adjusting entries for the discrepancies noted; and
The Treasurer’s Office cause the immediate remittance of all taxes withheld in accordance with existing rules and regulations issued by the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) and other implementing agencies to avoid the misuse of said funds and the incurrence of unnecessary expenses in the form of interest and surcharges.

Prospectively, the Accounting Office should be more mindful of the accounts used to journalize the transactions and the eventual result of such transactions in order to properly present the accounts balances in their normal balances.

The Municipal Accountant have already identified the cause of the errors and will effect the adjusting entries.

Prepare a reconciliation report of the reciprocal accounts and take up the necessary adjustment in the books of accounts of the respective funds; and

the OIC-Municipal Accountant shall:

shall check the completeness of the supporting documents before signing the Disbursement Voucher and refrain from processing the same with incomplete documents. Should there be lacking supporting documents for the claim, the same shall be returned;

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit the subject cash on treasury and faithfully comply with existing laws, rules and regulations in the collection and deposit of all income of the LGU to ensure that all monies received are fully accounted and properly recorded in the books of accounts and avoid possible loss and/or misuse of the scarce government funds.
The Municipal Mayor on the other hand (1) regularly monitor the deposits of these collections and (2) ensure that the Municipal Treasurer strictly comply with the existing regulation. Otherwise, institute disciplinary action on the continued practice of not depositing intact all collections.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Accountant:
maintain accurate and updated Subsidiary Ledgers to support the schedules of unliquidated cash advances and aging of outstanding receivables as of December 31, 2016 disclosing all the prior and current year’s unliquidated cash advances to properly ascertain the legality, validity, and propriety of the said account;
submit a certification that previous cash advance have been liquidated and accounted for in the books will be issued before a new cash advance is approved as required under existing rules and regulations on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances to ensure efficient and effective control of all cash advances; and
management faithfully adhere to sound management and fiscal administration laws, rules and regulations and the generally accepted accounting principles and practices especially in the recording of financial transactions and keeping of its accounts, to include, but not limited to keeping of accurate and updated ledgers, to safeguard against inaccurate or misleading information.

We recommended that:
the Disbursing Officer-Designate completely prepare all the necessary supporting documents to be attached to her claim before submitting the same to the Accounting Office; and
the Municipal Accountant check the completeness of the supporting documents before signing the Box “A” of the DV and refrain from processing DV with incomplete documents. Should there be lacking supporting documents for the claim, the same shall be returned to the claimant.

We recommended that the LCE refrain from incurring unnecessary and extravagant expenditures per Sections 4.0 & 6.0 of COA Circular 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012 and not in accordance with DILG MC No. 2011-59 dated April 25, 2011 and evidenced by substantial supporting documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer cause the immediate submission of unsubmitted DVs complete with the necessary supporting documents, including the CY 2015 to the Accounting Office. The Municipal Accountant, on the other hand, shall also submit these reports within ten (10) days after receipt to the Office of the Auditor for verification and post-audit.
If warranted, the Municipal Mayor may institute suspension of payment of their salaries until they have complied with the aforesaid requirements pursuant to Section 122 (2) of PD. 1445.

We recommended that management:
create an inventory committee to conduct the annual physical count of all the properties of the LGU;
upon completion of the physical inventory taking, the required Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment shall be prepared and submitted to the Municipal Accountant for reconciliation with accounting records; and
any difference noted in the process shall be recorded and/or accounted for to come up with realistic and fairly presented financial statements.

We recommended that Management:
require the Municipal Accountant to sign the DV and look into the completeness of the supporting documents. If the claim lacks the necessary supporting documents, it shall be returned to the claimant for compliance before payment shall be made;
instruct the Municipal Engineer to prepare Program of Work and Detailed Estimates before implementation of the project and submit an Inspection Report and Certificate of Completion after completion of any infrastructure projects; and
require the Municipal Budget Officer to approve the Obligation Request (ObR) in accordance with the guidelines set under Paragraph 3.0 of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1, dated April 13, 2011.

We recommended that management stop the practice of extending financial assistance as donation to an association and strictly adhere to the general principle that public funds shall be solely used for public purpose.

We recommended that management direct:
the MPSA to submit the requirements as mentioned in Section 4.4 of COA Circular No. 2007-001 dated October 25, 2007 to ensure the legality, validity and propriety of the subject claims; and
the Municipal Budget Officer to approve Obligation Request (ObR) in accordance with the guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1.

We recommended that management:
stop the grant of honoraria to personnel performing regular functions of the Municipality; and
adhere to existing rules and regulations in the grant of honoraria to ensure the legality of transactions. It is reminded that agency heads shall be held responsible and personally liable for honoraria payments not in accordance with the provisions of above-cited circular, without prejudice, however, to the refund of any excess payments by the personnel concerned. (Section 6.0, DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-2 dated October 1, 2007)

We recommended the submission of supporting documents and/or compliance of requirements to facilitate proper disposition of subject transaction documents and that DVs are processed and approved before payment to facilitate proper recording in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the PNP Chief be required to submit Driver’s Trip Ticket in prescribed form and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption as required in the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles. Refrain from paying fuel purchases through reimbursements.

We recommended the Municipal Treasurer or the concerned liable persons to refund the penalties incurred. For future transactions, the Municipal Treasurer should pay electric bills and other remittances on or before the due dates to preclude the incurrence of unnecessary expenditures in the form of penalties.

We recommended that management stop the remittance of government share for employees contribution to HDMF beyond the maximum amount of contribution. The remittance of government share for employees contribution should only be in the amount of P100.00 per employee per month unless otherwise the Board of Trustees fixed a higher amount.

We recommended that the accountable officer’s fidelity bond be increased in accordance to the Bureau of Treasury Circular No. 2-99 dated August 6, 2009 since her total actual cash advances is far beyond her bonded maximum accountability as shown in the above computation.

We recommended that the accountable officers/collectors liquidate their collections at the close of each day by preparing/accomplishing the RCDs and turn over the collections to the Liquidating Officer. Thereafter, the Liquidating Officer shall perform the procedures for the receipt and verification of collections and accomplish the RCDs and turn over intact the collections to the Municipal Treasurer.
Management ensure strict adherence to rules and regulations, appropriate disciplinary action shall be taken against accountable officers who fail to perform their functions and to comply with existing rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Liquidating Officer ensure the correctness of the balances and data contained in the RCDs; check the number of issuances and remaining balances of the accountable forms; and fill-up properly Sections C, D and the VERIFICATION AND ACKNOWLEDGMENT column of the RCD by indicating the amount in words and in figures received by the liquidating officer for easy monitoring of the movement of the accountable forms and the remittances of collections.

We recommended that the accountable officer faithfully adhere to the existing rules and regulations on the recording and reporting of the receipts and payments of all cash advances handled by her to facilitate monitoring and verification thereof.

We recommended that management faithfully comply the mandates and provisions of Republic Act No. 10121, particularly on the creation of the LDRRMO, which is tasked to perform several duties and functions with impartiality to strengthen the country's National DRRM System towards sustainable economic development, by mainstreaming the same in all national and local development processes.

We recommended that management to faithfully adhere to the provisions of RA 10121 especially on the utilization of the DRRM Fund so as not to defeat the purpose for which the law was created, that is, to address the root causes of vulnerabilities to disasters, strengthening the country’s institutional capacity for disaster risk reduction and management, and building resilience of local communities to disasters including climate change impacts.
It is also recommended that the required supporting documents be submitted immediately to facilitate timely evaluation, validation and proper disposition of subject transactions, and stop paying claims without complete supporting documents to avoid possible suspension and/or disallowance in audit.
